title: Federal judge faults environmental analysis for planned huge gold mine
summary: Yereth Rosen Alaska Beacon Opponents of the planned Donlin Gold mine in Western Alaska won a key victory on Monday when a federal judge ruled that regulators who granted a permit needed to build the project failed to properly consider the risks of a catastrophic release of mine waste. U.S. District Court Judge Sharon Gleason agreed with tribal government plaintiffs that argued the environmental study that led to federal permitting for the Donlin mine illegally omitted analysis of the impacts of a major tailings dam failure. “I think it’s a major v...

title: View 2024 Annual General Membership Meeting
summary: TAP TO VIEW 2024 ANNUAL GENERAL MEMBERSHIP MEETING Click the link above to view the 2024 Annual General Membership Meeting live at 10 a.m. Alaska Time on Saturday, Oct. 5, 2024. Participants may join the meeting 10 minutes prior or anytime during the meeting.

title: USDA to host Data Users’ Meeting to gather public input on statistical programs
summary: The U.S. Department of Agriculture’s National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) will hold its biannual Data Users’ Meeting on October 15-16 starting at 12 p.m. ET virtually via Zoom. title: The Triangle of Extraction in the Kola Peninsula
summary: Extractivism tied to the green transition is spreading in Saami lands on the Kola Peninsula, raising questions about power imbalances. The post The Triangle of Extraction in the Kola Peninsula appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .

title: This October, Get Familiar with Manufacturing, Women in Business, & Cybersecurity – We’re Here to Help!
summary: As small business owners and entrepreneurs in Alaska, the state’s diverse economic landscape presents unique opportunities and challenges. From manufacturing to cooperative businesses, from celebrating Women’s Business Month to addressing cybersecurity, Alaska’s small businesses have numerous avenues for growth and innovation this month. The Alaska Small Business Development Center (SBDC) provides support, offering advising, workshops, and a wealth of tools and resources to help businesses navigate these key areas.
